content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /job/.claude/skills/webflow

# Webflow Skill

Manage thestrategicveteran.com through the Webflow API v2. All scripts use `$WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN` for authentication and target site ID `66878d6a1082f3dca2c24ae6`.

## Prerequisites

- `WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN` environment variable must be set (available via `llm-secrets` skill)

## Scripts

### site-info.sh

Get site details (name, domain, last published date, etc).

```bash
skills/webflow/site-info.sh
```

### list-pages.sh

List all pages on the site with their IDs, titles, slugs, and SEO metadata.

```bash
skills/webflow/list-pages.sh
```

### list-collections.sh

List all CMS collections with their IDs, names, slugs, and field schemas.

```bash
skills/webflow/list-collections.sh
```

### list-collection-items.sh

List items in a CMS collection. Supports pagination via optional offset and limit args.

```bash
# List first 100 items (default)
skills/webflow/list-collection-items.sh <collection-id>

# Paginate: skip 100, get next 50
skills/webflow/list-collection-items.sh <collection-id> 100 50
```

### get-collection-item.sh

Get a single item from a collection by ID.

```bash
skills/webflow/get-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id>
```

### create-collection-item.sh

Create a new CMS item from a JSON file. The JSON structure depends on the collection's field schema.

```bash
# Create the JSON payload
cat > /tmp/new-item.json << 'EOF'
{
  "fieldData": {
    "name": "My New Item",
    "slug": "my-new-item",
    "some-field": "value"
  }
}
EOF

skills/webflow/create-collection-item.sh <collection-id> /tmp/new-item.json
```

### update-collection-item.sh

Update an existing CMS item. Only include fields you want to change.

```bash
cat > /tmp/update.json << 'EOF'
{
  "fieldData": {
    "name": "Updated Title"
  }
}
EOF

skills/webflow/update-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id> /tmp/update.json
```

### publish-site.sh

Publish all pending changes to the live site. Use after creating or updating CMS items.

```bash
skills/webflow/publish-site.sh
```

## Site Reference

- **Site ID:** `66878d6a1082f3dca2c24ae6`
- **Domain:** thestrategicveteran.com
- **Site map:** See `data/webflow-site-map.md` for full page/collection inventory

   235→## Technical Notes for AI Agents
   236→
   237→### Webflow API Access
   238→
   239→The Webflow skill scripts are at `skills/webflow/`. Before using CMS read/write scripts, the API token must be updated with the required scopes (see API Token Status section above).
   240→
   241→Available scripts:
   242→- `skills/webflow/site-info.sh` -- Get site details (requires `sites:read`)
   243→- `skills/webflow/list-pages.sh` -- List all pages (requires `pages:read`)
   244→- `skills/webflow/list-collections.sh` -- List CMS collections with field schemas (requires `cms:read`)
   245→- `skills/webflow/list-collection-items.sh <collection-id>` -- List items in a collection (requires `cms:read`)
   246→- `skills/webflow/get-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id>` -- Get single item (requires `cms:read`)
   247→- `skills/webflow/create-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <json-file>` -- Create CMS item (requires `cms:write`)
   248→- `skills/webflow/update-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id> <json-file>` -- Update CMS item (requires `cms:write`)
   249→- `skills/webflow/publish-site.sh` -- Publish site (requires `sites:write`)
   250→
   251→### Collection IDs
   252→
   253→Collection IDs are not yet known because the API token lacks `cms:read` scope. Once the token is updated, run `skills/webflow/list-collections.sh` to get collection IDs and field schemas. The podcast episodes collection ID will be needed for any CMS operations.

   257→Page IDs are not yet known because the API token lacks `pages:read` scope. Once the token is updated, run `skills/webflow/list-pages.sh` to get page IDs needed for page-level operations.
   258→
   259→### Making Changes
   260→
   261→1. Use `list-collections.sh` to get collection ID

   264→4. Use `create-collection-item.sh` or `update-collection-item.sh`
   265→5. Use `publish-site.sh` to push changes live

## API Token Status

The current Webflow API token (`$WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN`) is an OAuth token with **limited scopes**. It currently only has webhook access. The following scopes are **missing** and need to be added in the Webflow dashboard:

| Scope | Required For |
|-------|-------------|
| `sites:read` | site-info.sh |

| `sites:write` | publish-site.sh (POST) |

To fix: Go to Webflow Dashboard > Site Settings > Apps & Integrations > Edit the API token and add the missing scopes. Or generate a new Site API Token (legacy tokens have all scopes by default for their site).

---

## Pages (8 total)

## CMS Collections

### Podcast Episodes Collection

This is the primary (and likely only) CMS collection on the site. Episodes are displayed on the `/podcast` page and each has an individual page at `/podcast/{slug}`.

**Total episodes in sitemap:** 120
**Total episodes reported by podcast platforms:** 158+ (meaning ~38 episodes exist in feeds but not on the website)

#### Collection Fields (observed from episode pages)

| Field | Type | Required | Notes |
|-------|------|----------|-------|
| Name | Plain Text | Yes | Guest name, used as page heading |
| Slug | Auto-generated | Yes | URL path segment. Inconsistent conventions (see below) |
| Portrait | Image | Yes | Guest headshot photo. Alt text template has typo: "{Name}portraitportriat" |
| Release Date | Date | Yes | Formatted "Month DD, YYYY" |
| Episode Description | Rich Text | No | Full episode description with paragraphs. Only populated on ~20% of episodes |
| Chapter Timestamps | Rich Text | No | Formatted timestamp list (e.g., "0:00 - Introduction"). Only on well-populated episodes |
| Resources | Rich Text / Links | No | Guest links, company URLs, social profiles |
| Episode Thumbnail | Image | No | Separate from portrait (e.g., "EP.017.jpg") |

#### Slug Conventions (inconsistent)

The episode slugs follow multiple conventions:

1. **Capitalized names** (47 episodes): `Aaron-Hill`, `Scott-Deluzio`, `Tyler-Goble`
2. **Lowercase names** (62 episodes): `david-befort`, `chad-rarie`, `brian-moss`
3. **Truncated/auto-generated** (11 episodes): `ba`, `c`, `d`, `ja`, `jo`, `l`, `m`, `n`, `pe`, `st`, `t`
4. **Suffixed duplicates**: `Tyler-Schmoker-79609`, `Walt-Morgan-a8549`, `d-82209`, `d-9b552`, `m-3ad36`, `m-65d03`, `m-d120e`, `christopher-maritni-ae6fd`

The truncated slugs (`ba`, `c`, `d`, etc.) appear to be auto-generated when proper slugs weren't provided. These are problematic for SEO and usability.

#### Full Episode List (120 episodes in sitemap)

**Capitalized slug format (47):**
Aaron Hill, Adam Bird, Alex Sosa, Anthony Bourke, Bill Kiefer, Christopher Phillips, Daren Pallatina, Darrell Williams, Dave Albin, David Fivecoat, David Jensen, Deric Keller, Dylan Dail, Edwin Carrion, Evan Poling, George Boyd, George Emmerson Hayworth, Holly Taylor, J Mach, Jack Daly, Jake Ray, Jay Patterson, Jeremy Bourgeois, Jim Fuhs, Juan Alvarado Jr, Kristen Morgan, Laura Kandewen, Lawson Latimer, Mark Gray, Mark Toner, Murray Beaulieu, Paul Mackiewicz, Rachelle Smith, Rich Hardin, Rich Ingram, Rich LaMonica, Ron Jansen, Scott Deluzio, Shawn Welsh, Tom Spooner, Tyler Goble, Tyler Nelson, Tyler Schmoker (x2), Walt Morgan, Wendi Wray, Zack Walker

**Lowercase slug format (62):**
Adrian Massey, Albert Winks, Alex Tessier, Alexis Benjamin, Antony Towndrow, Aren Brandfass, Arnold Beekes, Ben Vernon, Benjamin Parmele, Brandon Jenkins, Brandon Lovingier, Brent Bowers, Brian Fleming, Brian Moss, Chad Rarie, Christopher Maritni, Coach Rob, Damon Darnall, Darlene Greene, Dave Rosenberg, David Befort, David Pere, Douglas Katz, Doyle Glass, Ed Paracut, Eric Donoho, G. Michael Hopf, Georgia Arts, Ian Chamandy, Jackie Frederick, James Artman, John Dailey, John Tansill, Jonathan Sessa, Justin Boyum, Justin Henderson, Lauren Frazier (Part 2), Logan Spiewak, Manuel Martinez, Mark Booher, Mark Messina & Chris Ingram, Markos Eugenios, Matt Payne, Nathan Mechikoff, Otis McGregor, Rhiannon Gonzalez, Rich Mulder, Richard Kauffman, Richard Walsh, Rod Blunk, Ron Nussbaum, Scottie Schneider, Shawn Burnah, Steven Crane, Tim Thomas, Tom Collins, Tony Bozzi

**Truncated slugs (11):**
`ba` (Natalie Schibell?), `c` (Chad Hufford?), `d` (3 entries), `ja`, `jo`, `l`, `m` (4 entries), `n`, `pe`, `st`, `t`

**Episodes on homepage but NOT in sitemap (3):**
David Zallo, Rich Spalla, Rex Enriquez

#### Episode Page Content Quality

Episodes fall into two tiers:

1. **Well-populated (~20%):** Full description, chapter timestamps, resource links, guest bio. Example: David Befort (9 chapters, resource links, full narrative)
2. **Minimal (~80%):** Guest name, headshot, release date only. No description, no chapters, no resources. Example: Scott Deluzio (name, photo, date only)

---

## Site Structure Notes

### Navigation Structure
```
Header Nav: Podcast | Newsletter | Shop | About | Contact
Footer: Social links (YouTube, X, Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn)
```

### Content Organization Patterns

1. **Single CMS collection:** The site uses one CMS collection (Podcast Episodes) that powers both the `/podcast` grid page and individual `/podcast/{slug}` pages.
2. **No blog:** Despite the audit recommending one, no blog collection or pages exist.
3. **No store:** The "Shop" nav link points to `#` (placeholder).
4. **Static pages only:** Homepage, About, Newsletter, Contact, Guest, Guest Form, and Search are all static Webflow pages (not CMS-powered).
5. **No embedded audio/video:** Episode pages don't embed players. Listeners are directed to Apple Podcasts, YouTube, Spotify, and Podbean externally.
6. **No SEO metadata:** None of the pages have meta descriptions or OG tags set.
7. **Copyright date stale:** Footer says 2024.
8. **Design credit:** "The Brand Chef" credited in footer.

### Collection-to-Page Mapping

| Collection | Page | Relationship |
|-----------|------|-------------|
| Podcast Episodes | `/podcast` | Collection list (grid of all episodes) |
| Podcast Episodes | `/podcast/{slug}` | Collection item template (individual episode page) |

### Image Patterns
- Guest portraits stored on Webflow CDN (`cdn.prod.website-files.com`)
- Episode thumbnails use naming like "EP.017.jpg"
- Portrait alt text template has a typo: `{Name}portraitportriat`

### Missing Functionality
- No audio/video embedding on episode pages
- No episode search or filtering by topic/guest
- No episode count displayed anywhere
- No "as seen in" press section
- No social proof on homepage beyond Marquis Who's Who badge

---

## Recommendations Context

This site belongs to **Adam Peters / The Strategic Veteran** (TSV Studios LLC). A comprehensive website audit exists at `data/website-audit.md` (dated 2026-03-04) that identified significant gaps between Adam's actual presence and what the website communicates.

### Missing Pages That Need to Be Built

Based on the audit findings, the following pages do not exist and should be created:

| Page | Suggested Slug | Purpose |
|------|---------------|---------|
| Speaking | `/speaking` | Past events, topics, rate card, booking CTA. Adam speaks at VETSOCIAL25, U of Chicago/Boeing summit, Military CreatorCon, etc. |
| Coaching | `/coaching` | One-on-one transition coaching offerings, testimonials, TidyCal booking integration |
| AI Consulting | `/ai-consulting` | Fractional CAIO services, Alset Technologies connection, AI implementation offerings |
| Community (The Stronghold) | `/community` | The Stronghold veteran community description and join flow (now free) |
| Press & Media | `/press` | Magazine features (Bold Journey, CanvasRebel x2, Umbrella Local Heroes), podcast guest appearances, media kit |
| Episode Archive | `/episodes` | Searchable, filterable directory of all 158+ episodes with embedded players |
| Full Bio | `/about/adam` | Adam's complete transformation story: combat deployments, medical retirement, weight loss, plant medicine, founding TSV |
| Amakaya | `/amakaya` | Partnership with Amakaya Ayahuasca Retreat in Peru, Adam's healing journey, resources for veterans |
| Blog | `/blog` | Regular content for SEO, repurposed podcast insights, transition tips, AI articles |
| Store | `/store` | Audiobook, Ramshorn Coffee partnership, merchandise, digital products |

### Key Data Points for Building Missing Pages

- **Episodes published:** 158+ (per podcast platforms), 120 on website
- **Press features:** Bold Journey (Jul 2024), CanvasRebel x2 (Jul 2024, Dec 2024), Umbrella Local Heroes (Jul 2024)
- **Speaking events:** VETSOCIAL25, University of Chicago/Boeing Summit (Oct 2025), Military CreatorCon Dallas, Tampa events
- **Ventures:** TSV (founder), Alset Technologies (CTO), Amakaya Retreat (partner)
- **Titles:** Founder, CAIO, CTO, Veteran Transition Architect
- **Social:** LinkedIn (~16K-23K followers), YouTube, X, Instagram, Facebook
- **Booking:** TidyCal at tidycal.com/thestrategicveteran
- **Email:** support@thestrategicveteran.com
- **Sponsors:** Ramshorn Coffee (code: THESTRATEGICVET), Endure By Faith (partner)
- **Newsletter:** 339 subscribers, "Your Weekly Blueprint For Success"

---

## Technical Notes for AI Agents

### Webflow API Access

The Webflow skill scripts are at `skills/webflow/`. Before using CMS read/write scripts, the API token must be updated with the required scopes (see API Token Status section above).

Available scripts:
- `skills/webflow/site-info.sh` -- Get site details (requires `sites:read`)
- `skills/webflow/list-pages.sh` -- List all pages (requires `pages:read`)
- `skills/webflow/list-collections.sh` -- List CMS collections with field schemas (requires `cms:read`)
- `skills/webflow/list-collection-items.sh <collection-id>` -- List items in a collection (requires `cms:read`)
- `skills/webflow/get-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id>` -- Get single item (requires `cms:read`)
- `skills/webflow/create-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <json-file>` -- Create CMS item (requires `cms:write`)
- `skills/webflow/update-collection-item.sh <collection-id> <item-id> <json-file>` -- Update CMS item (requires `cms:write`)
- `skills/webflow/publish-site.sh` -- Publish site (requires `sites:write`)

### Collection IDs

Collection IDs are not yet known because the API token lacks `cms:read` scope. Once the token is updated, run `skills/webflow/list-collections.sh` to get collection IDs and field schemas. The podcast episodes collection ID will be needed for any CMS operations.

### Page IDs

Page IDs are not yet known because the API token lacks `pages:read` scope. Once the token is updated, run `skills/webflow/list-pages.sh` to get page IDs needed for page-level operations.

### Making Changes

1. Use `list-collections.sh` to get collection ID
2. Use `list-collection-items.sh` to browse existing items
3. Create JSON payload matching the collection's field schema
4. Use `create-collection-item.sh` or `update-collection-item.sh`
5. Use `publish-site.sh` to push changes live

### Key Fixes Needed (Quick Wins)

1. Fix portrait alt text typo: `portraitportriat` -> `portrait`
2. Fix truncated episode slugs (11 episodes with slugs like `ba`, `c`, `d`)
3. Add meta descriptions to all pages
4. Update copyright to 2025/2026
5. Fix Shop nav link (currently `#`)
6. Populate episode descriptions for the ~80% that are empty

file.content:
# thestrategicveteran.com — Full Audit Report

**Date:** 2026-03-04
**Prepared by:** Autonomous Audit Agent

---

## Executive Summary

Adam Peters has built a significant and growing presence in the veteran transition space — 158 published podcast episodes, multiple magazine features, conference speaking engagements, three active ventures (The Strategic Veteran, Alset Technologies, Amakaya Retreat), and a strong LinkedIn following. However, thestrategicveteran.com dramatically under-represents this reality. The website is essentially a static brochure from mid-2024 that has not kept pace with Adam's rapid evolution as a thought leader, tech founder, and public speaker. This audit identifies every gap between what the world knows about Adam Peters and what his website communicates.

---

## Part 1: What We Found Online (Adam Peters Web Presence)

### 1.1 Adam Peters — Who He Is (According to the Internet)

**Core Identity:**
- 13-year U.S. Army infantry veteran (2003–2015)
- Four combat deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan
- Medically retired
- Born and raised in southern Indiana
- Currently based in Willacoochee, GA (per Umbrella Local Heroes, July 2024)
- Combat veteran turned community builder, tech founder, and integration guide

**Current Titles (per VETSOCIAL25 and University of Chicago Summit 2025):**
- Founder, The Strategic Veteran
- CTO, Alset Technologies
- Partner, Amakaya Ayahuasca Retreat
- CAIO (Chief AI Officer)
- "Veteran Transition Architect"

**Personal Transformation Story (well-documented across multiple interviews):**
- After military service, fell into depression, gained significant weight, struggled with alcohol dependency (later replaced with food/sugar addiction and cannabis use)
- Underwent nine surgeries
- Pivotal ayahuasca ceremony sparked radical transformation
- Lost almost 100 pounds (starting July 7, ~2023)
- Founded TSV in November 2023
- Podcast launched May 1, 2024

### 1.2 Press Coverage & Magazine Features

| Publication | Date | Title/Topic |
|---|---|---|
| **Bold Journey Magazine** | July 2024 | "Meet Adam Peters" — resilience, ayahuasca transformation, building TSV |
| **CanvasRebel Magazine** (1st feature) | July 2024 | Interview on resilience, self-awareness, networking |
| **CanvasRebel Magazine** (2nd feature) | December 2024 | Execution story, pivot from paid Stronghold to free, AI automation launch |
| **Umbrella Local Heroes** | July 2024 | "Empowering Veterans through Strategic Community Building" |
| **Endure By Faith** | N/A | Listed as partner podcast |

### 1.3 Podcast: The Strategic Veteran

**Stats (per Rephonic, as of audit date):**
- **158 episodes published** (launched ~2 years ago, first episode May 1, 2024)
- Apple Podcasts ID: 1743039436
- Available on: Spotify, Apple Podcasts, YouTube, Megaphone
- Hosted via: Heroes Media Group / Megaphone
- Format: Long-form interviews, typically 1–2+ hours

**Podcast Description (from Apple Podcasts):**
> "Welcome to The Strategic Veteran, a raw and unfiltered journey into military transition. Host Adam Peters challenges the status quo by sharing authentic stories of veterans navigating life after service while advocating for real systemic change in how our military prepares service members for civilian life. Each episode dives deep into the human transition experience — from the struggles that many face but few discuss to the transformative moments that shape new paths forward. Through honest conversations with fellow veterans, Adam explores the challenges of identity, purpose, and belonging that come with hanging up the uniform. More than just a podcast, The Strategic Veteran is a movement."

**YouTube Channel Description:**
> "The Strategic Veteran | Military Transition, Identity, and Purpose — A long-form podcast for transitioning service members, veterans, and military families navigating life after the uniform."

**Known Recent Guests (from Apple Podcasts/Spotify, most recent first):**
- Joshua Palassis (Feb 23, 2026) — Canadian Army vet, brand strategist
- Peter Pollock (Feb 23, 2026) — Retired USAF Colonel, leadership coach
- Rand Timmerman (Feb 9, 2026) — Vietnam War Marine, AT thru-hiker at 70
- David Puri
- Benjamin Parmele
- Georgia Lee Arts
- Jason Anderson
- Matt Payne
- Buffy Payne
- Chad Rarie
- Richard Walsh
- Jay Patterson — Navy submarine CPO, Wolfpack Leadership founder
- David Befort — A-10 pilot, Max Performance Financial
- Chad Cooper — mental toughness, global impact
- And 140+ more guests

**Podcast Platforms/Feeds:**
- Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd
- Apple: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@thestrategicveteran
- Megaphone: https://feeds.megaphone.fm/the-strategic-veteran

**Book/Audiobook:**
- Listed on Amazon.co.uk as "The Strategic Veteran" by Adam Peters (ASIN: B0FLGKDF6R) — appears to be an audiobook compilation of podcast episodes

### 1.4 Guest Appearances on Other Podcasts

Adam has been a guest on multiple shows, significantly expanding his reach beyond his own audience:

| Show | Date | Platform |
|---|---|---|
| **Thought Forge Network** (Manuel Martinez) | Sept 2, 2024 | Spotify — 2hr 11min episode |
| **HR Payroll 2.0** (Pete & Julie) | Sept 2025 | Apple Podcasts — discussed ayahuasca, AI/automation, Alset Technologies |
| **Civvies Podcast** | Sept 11, 2025 | YouTube — "Strategic Veteran Podcast Host's Unconventional Journey to Healing" |
| **VET S.O.S. Podcast** (Military Transition Resources Show) | Nov 12, 2025 | YouTube — Veterans Day special |
| **Unknown show** (Healing, Transition & Truth) | Aug 28, 2025 | YouTube — "The Strategic Veteran: Adam Peters on Healing, Transition & Truth" |

### 1.5 Speaking Engagements & Conferences

| Event | Date | Role/Title Listed |
|---|---|---|
| **VETSOCIAL25 LIVE** (Veteran Social Summit) | 2025 | Speaker — "Founder, CAIO, and Veteran Transition Architect" |
| **2025 Military Life Cycle and Transition Summit** (University of Chicago / Boeing) | Oct 2-3, 2025 | Solo Presentation (2:15 PM slot) — "Adam Peters, Founder, The Strategic Veteran; CTO, Alset Technologies; Partner, Amakaya Ayahuasca Retreat" |
| **The Speakers Collaborative** | Ongoing (mentioned Dec 2024) | Currently developing public speaking skills through this program |

### 1.6 Ventures & Affiliations

**The Strategic Veteran (TSV)**
- Founded: November 2023
- Podcast launched: May 1, 2024
- Core offerings: Podcast, The Stronghold community (now free), one-on-one coaching, newsletter
- Revenue model pivoted: initially paid Stronghold ($450 MRR), made free to focus on AI automation services
- Registered on Heroes Media Group network for podcast production
- Ramshorn Coffee discount code "THESTRATEGICVET" — sponsor/affiliate relationship
- Partnership with Endure By Faith

**Alset Technologies** (alsettechnologies.com)
- Adam's role: CTO (per University of Chicago summit listing)
- Tagline: "Accelerate your business with AI. Our military-inspired approach delivers AI solutions that transform your business operations."
- NOTE: This is NOT "Alset AI Ventures" (TSXV:GPUS) — that is a separate Canadian company run by Adam Ingrao. The naming similarity is coincidental.

**Amakaya Retreat** (amakaya.org)
- Adam's role: Partner
- Boutique ayahuasca retreat center in the Peruvian Amazon
- Small groups, safe environment, highly professional team
- Adam's personal bio on their site: "Adam is a 13-year Army infantry veteran who served from 2003 to 2015, including four combat deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan. After being medically retired, Adam found himself adrift, carrying an invisible burden and struggling..."
- Blends indigenous wisdom with modern scientifically-grounded approaches to healing

**Heroes Media Group** (heroesmediagroup.com)
- Adam Peters' podcast is produced/distributed through HMG
- HMG is run by Adam Bird (different person), founded 2015
- Digital media/publishing platform for veteran-focused content
- Based in Washington, DC

### 1.7 Social Media Presence

| Platform | Handle/URL | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **LinkedIn** (personal) | linkedin.com/in/thestrategicveteran | Primary platform, strong following (23K+ followers per one snippet) |
| **LinkedIn** (company) | linkedin.com/company/the-strategic-veteran | Active |
| **X/Twitter** | @thestrategicvet | Active |
| **Instagram** | @pete0585 | Active |
| **Facebook** (personal) | facebook.com/pete.peters.39 | Active |
| **Facebook** (page) | facebook.com/profile.php?id=61563037727718 | Active |
| **YouTube** | youtube.com/@thestrategicveteran | Active — podcast video channel |
| **TidyCal** | tidycal.com/thestrategicveteran | Booking link |

### 1.8 Testimonials & Social Proof Found Online

**LinkedIn recommendations (from search snippets):**
- "He's created a national movement grounded in mentorship, real talk, and actionable tools that give veterans the support we often struggle to find after taking the uniform off."
- "What sets Adam apart is his heart. He's not in this for credit — he's in it to save lives, restore purpose, and reconnect warriors to something bigger than themselves."
- "Through his podcast, automation systems, and deep transformation coaching, he's helping people take back control of their story."
- "Adam Peters isn't just a fellow veteran — he's my brother in arms and one of the most purpose-driven leaders I know."
- From Paul W. Roden III, LTC, DML, TS-SCI
- From Scott H. Stalker, S2 - Stalker Solutions LLC (23K followers)

**Podcast reviews (from Apple Podcasts):**
- Scott DeLuzio: "Incredible host with a great show for military veterans and transitioning service members."
- Lawson Latimer: "As a veteran myself, it's comforting knowing that people like Adam and his guest are in the world crushing it."
- "What a great podcast! Adam is a great host who can move the conversation exactly where it needs to go."

### 1.9 Key Programs & Initiatives Mentioned Online

- **The Stronghold** — veteran community within TSV, focused on mental health and personal growth (originally paid, now free)
- **One-on-one coaching sessions** — personalized veteran transition support
- **AI automation solutions** — announced as upcoming service line
- **Courses** — marketing, copywriting, graphic design for veteran entrepreneurs
- **Newsletter** — "Your Weekly Blueprint For Success"
- **ACP (American Corporate Partners)** mentorship — Adam participated as a protege
- **Ramshorn Coffee** — sponsor/affiliate partnership (discount code THESTRATEGICVET)

---

## Part 2: Current Website State (Page by Page)

### 2.1 Homepage (thestrategicveteran.com/)

**What's there:**
- Hero section with Adam's intro: "Hi, I'm Adam Peters. After 13 years in the military, I faced significant challenges when trying to transition to civilian life."
- "The fire that lights my soul" narrative
- Promise of "in-depth and comprehensive services" — personalized career guidance, mental health support
- "Take us anywhere you go" podcast CTA — links to podcast apps
- Generic tagline: "We empower veterans and transitioning service members with curated resources, expert guidance, and a supportive community — all under one roof."

**Issues:**
- No mention of 158 episodes or the podcast's reach
- No mention of Alset Technologies, Amakaya, or AI consulting
- No mention of speaking engagements or press features
- No "as seen in" or social proof section
- No clear call-to-action beyond vague "services"
- No episode count, no guest highlights, no testimonials on homepage
- The tagline "Unfuck Your Transition" (if used) is nowhere on the site
- Homepage reads like a generic veteran support org, not a media brand with 158 episodes and a growing movement
- No current titles: CAIO, CTO, Speaker — just "founder"

### 2.2 About Page (thestrategicveteran.com/about)

**What's there:**
- "Our Mission" section
- Brief founder bio: "The Strategic Veteran was founded by Adam Peters. After 13 years of service, Adam found the transition to civilian life full of pitfalls, untrustworthy 'gurus', and of course, thousands of courses and communities promising everything under the sun."
- Mentions curbing veteran suicide as ultimate purpose

**Issues:**
- Extremely thin — just a few sentences
- No personal transformation story (100 lb weight loss, ayahuasca, nine surgeries)
- No mention of combat deployments (4 deployments to Iraq/Afghanistan)
- No current ventures listed (Alset Technologies, Amakaya)
- No press features listed
- No speaking credentials
- No photos beyond what appears to be a basic layout
- No mention of "Army infantry" specifically — just "13 years of service"
- Missing: The Stronghold, coaching offerings, AI automation work
- No timeline or milestones (founded Nov 2023, podcast launched May 2024, 158 episodes by 2026)

### 2.3 Podcast Page (thestrategicveteran.com/podcast)

**What's there:**
- Title: "Real Stories, Real Strategies, Real Success — Hosted by Adam Peters"
- Testimonial carousel with 8 reviews from LinkedIn and Apple Podcasts (Haley Lytle, Jonathan Sessa, Kyle Kushner, Scott DeLuzio, blownupguy, Lawson Latimer, Brad Tachi, Jason Zeller)
- Appears to have podcast player embed or links

**Issues:**
- No episode count anywhere (158 episodes is a major achievement — should be prominent)
- No episode archive or searchable list on this page
- No "listen on" badges for Spotify, Apple, YouTube, Megaphone
- No featured/popular episodes highlighted
- No guest directory or categorization
- No description of the show's format or what to expect
- The testimonials are good but could be stronger with numbers/metrics

### 2.4 Individual Episode Pages (e.g., /podcast/david-befort)

**What's there:**
- Episode title, release date, full description
- Chapter timestamps
- Guest bio, resources mentioned
- Social follow links
- "Like, Comment, and Subscribe" CTA

**Issues:**
- Good format! These individual pages are well-structured
- However, there's no master episode directory linking to them all
- No way to browse/discover episodes from the main podcast page
- No search or filter by topic

### 2.5 Newsletter Page (thestrategicveteran.com/newsletter)

**What's there:**
- "Your Weekly Blueprint For Success"
- Signup form (name + email)
- "Read by top executives at:" with logos (PGA of America, Diamond Sharp Capital, Walking Warrior, Veteran Roadmap, SHU)

**Issues:**
- "Read by top executives at" — is this still accurate and current?
- No sample content or archive of past newsletters
- No subscriber count or social proof beyond logos
- Minimal page with just a form

### 2.6 Contact Page (thestrategicveteran.com/contact)

**What's there:**
- "Looking for answers?" header
- Basic contact form (name, email, message)
- TSV logo image

**Issues:**
- No phone number listed (912-656-1917 is mentioned in other press)
- No email address displayed (support@thestrategicveteran.com exists per interviews)
- No social media links
- No booking link (TidyCal exists: tidycal.com/thestrategicveteran)
- No indication of response time
- Very bare-bones

### 2.7 Pages That DON'T EXIST But Should

Based on Adam's actual activities and online presence, the following pages are completely absent from the website:

1. **Speaking Page** — Adam is speaking at VETSOCIAL25, University of Chicago/Boeing summit, and training through The Speakers Collaborative. No speaking page, no booking info, no past events listed.

2. **AI Consulting / Alset Technologies Page** — Adam is CTO of Alset Technologies and identifies as CAIO. This entire venture has zero presence on TSV's website.

3. **Amakaya / Plant Medicine Page** — Adam is a Partner at Amakaya Ayahuasca Retreat and openly discusses plant medicine's role in his healing. This is central to his story but absent from the website.

4. **Coaching/Services Page** — One-on-one coaching is mentioned across every interview but there's no dedicated services or coaching page with pricing, booking, or testimonials.

5. **The Stronghold Community Page** — This is TSV's community platform, mentioned extensively in press. No page, no signup link, no description.

6. **Press/Media Page** — Two Bold Journey features, two CanvasRebel features, Umbrella Local Heroes, multiple podcast guest appearances. None of this is on the website.

7. **Episode Archive/Directory** — 158 episodes exist but there's no browsable archive, no guest directory, no topic categorization.

8. **Blog/Resources** — No blog, no written content, no SEO-building articles.

9. **Book/Store Page** — Amazon listing exists for The Strategic Veteran audiobook. Not mentioned on website.

10. **Adam's Full Bio Page** — The about page is about TSV's mission, not Adam's full story. His transformation narrative, credentials, combat service, weight loss, plant medicine journey — all of this deserves its own page.

---

## Part 3: Gap Analysis — Reality vs. Website

### 3.1 Identity Gap

| What the Internet Says | What the Website Says |
|---|---|
| "Combat veteran, 4 deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan" | "13 years in the military" |
| "Army infantry veteran, medically retired" | Just "military" |
| "Founder, CAIO, CTO of Alset Technologies, Partner at Amakaya" | Just "founder of TSV" |
| "158 episodes published, growing movement" | No episode count |
| "Featured in Bold Journey, CanvasRebel, Umbrella Local Heroes" | No press mentions |
| "Speaker at VETSOCIAL25, University of Chicago/Boeing" | No speaking mentions |
| "Lost 100 lbs, overcame alcohol dependency, transformed via ayahuasca" | "I faced significant challenges" |
| "Building AI automation solutions, CTO role" | No AI/tech mentions |
| "The Stronghold community" | Not mentioned |
| "Available on Spotify, Apple, YouTube, Megaphone" | Vague "listen on your favorite app" |

### 3.2 Content Gap

| Content That Exists in the World | Present on Website? |
|---|---|
| 158 podcast episodes with detailed show notes | Only individual episode pages (no archive/browse) |
| 2 Bold Journey magazine features | No |
| 2 CanvasRebel magazine features | No |
| Umbrella Local Heroes interview | No |
| 5+ podcast guest appearances on other shows | No |
| VETSOCIAL25 speaker listing | No |
| U of Chicago/Boeing summit presentation | No |
| Amazon book/audiobook listing | No |
| Ramshorn Coffee partnership | No |
| Endure By Faith partnership | No |
| Heroes Media Group network affiliation | No |
| Amakaya Retreat partnership | No |
| Alset Technologies CTO role | No |
| ACP mentorship participation | No |
| Transformation story (weight loss, plant medicine, recovery) | Barely hinted at |
| The Stronghold community | No |
| Coaching services/booking | No |
| AI/automation service offerings | No |

### 3.3 Credibility Gap

The website presents Adam as a veteran who helps other veterans. The internet presents Adam as:
- A **media figure** with 158 episodes and a growing audience
- A **tech founder** building AI solutions for businesses
- A **speaker** at major veteran and academic conferences
- A **healing advocate** partnered with an ayahuasca retreat center
- A **published author** (audiobook on Amazon)
- A **certified/trained CAIO** working in AI consulting
- A **mentor** through ACP and direct coaching

The website captures approximately 10% of his actual credibility.

### 3.4 SEO Gap

The website has minimal content for search engines to index:
- Only 5 discoverable pages (home, about, podcast, newsletter, contact)
- Individual episode pages exist but aren't browsable
- No blog content
- No keyword-rich pages for "veteran transition coaching," "AI consulting for veterans," "veteran podcast," "ayahuasca veteran healing," "military keynote speaker"
- None of the magazine features link back to dedicated pages on TSV
- Massive missed opportunity for organic search traffic

---

## Part 4: Pages That Need to Be Built

### 4.1 Critical New Pages

1. **Adam's Story (Full Bio Page)**
   - Combat service details (Army infantry, 4 deployments, Iraq/Afghanistan, 2003-2015)
   - Medically retired
   - Personal transformation: depression, weight gain, 9 surgeries, alcohol dependency, plant medicine awakening, 100 lb weight loss
   - Building TSV from 93 LinkedIn followers to 158-episode podcast
   - Current roles: Founder TSV, CTO Alset Technologies, Partner Amakaya

2. **Speaking & Events Page**
   - Past speaking: VETSOCIAL25, U of Chicago/Boeing Summit
   - Topics available for speaking
   - Booking information / inquiry form
   - Speaker one-sheet download

3. **AI Consulting / Alset Technologies Page**
   - What Alset Technologies does
   - Adam's CAIO / CTO credentials
   - Service offerings
   - How military discipline translates to AI transformation
   - Link to alsettechnologies.com

4. **Coaching & Services Page**
   - One-on-one coaching offerings
   - Who it's for (transitioning vets, veteran entrepreneurs)
   - Testimonials from coaching clients
   - Booking link (TidyCal integration)
   - Pricing or inquiry process

5. **The Stronghold Community Page**
   - What The Stronghold is
   - How to join (now free)
   - Community features and benefits
   - Testimonials from members

6. **Press & Media Page**
   - All magazine features with links and pull quotes
   - Podcast guest appearances (with links)
   - Logos of publications featured in
   - Media inquiry contact information
   - Downloadable press kit / headshots

7. **Episode Archive / Guest Directory**
   - Searchable/browsable list of all 158 episodes
   - Filter by topic, branch of service, guest type
   - Featured episodes section
   - "Start Here" recommendations for new listeners

8. **Amakaya / Plant Medicine & Healing Page**
   - Adam's personal ayahuasca story
   - Partnership with Amakaya Retreat
   - Link to amakaya.org
   - Resources for veterans interested in plant medicine
   - Disclaimers and responsible guidance

9. **Blog / Resources Section**
   - Regular written content for SEO
   - Transition tips, AI insights, entrepreneurship advice
   - Repurposed podcast content (transcripts, key takeaways)

10. **Store / Products Page**
    - Audiobook / book link (Amazon)
    - Ramshorn Coffee partnership with discount code
    - Any future merchandise or digital products

---

## Part 5: Prioritized Fix List

### Quick Wins (Low Effort, High Impact)

1. **Update episode count everywhere** — Add "158+ episodes" prominently on homepage and podcast page
2. **Add "As Seen In" section to homepage** — Bold Journey, CanvasRebel, Umbrella Local Heroes logos
3. **Add podcast platform badges** — Spotify, Apple, YouTube, Megaphone with direct links on podcast page and homepage
4. **Update Adam's title** — Change from just "Founder" to "Founder & Host, The Strategic Veteran | CTO, Alset Technologies | Partner, Amakaya Retreat"
5. **Add contact details to Contact page** — Email (support@thestrategicveteran.com), phone (912-656-1917), social media links, TidyCal booking link
6. **Add social media links in footer/header** — LinkedIn, X, Instagram, Facebook, YouTube are all active but not linked on site
7. **Add podcast description on podcast page** — Use the Apple Podcasts / Spotify description text
8. **Add testimonials to homepage** — Pull from LinkedIn recommendations (the LTC and 23K-follower endorsements are powerful)
9. **Update the About page** — Expand from 3 sentences to include combat deployments, transformation story, current roles

### Medium Lifts (Moderate Effort, High Impact)

10. **Build Episode Archive page** — Browsable directory of all 158 episodes with search/filter
11. **Build Press & Media page** — Aggregate all magazine features, podcast appearances, and speaking events
12. **Build Coaching/Services page** — Dedicated page with offerings, testimonials, and TidyCal booking
13. **Build The Stronghold landing page** — Explain the community, how to join, what members get
14. **Build Adam's Full Bio page** — The full transformation story with photos and timeline
15. **Build Speaking page** — Past events, topics, booking inquiry
16. **Create blog infrastructure** — Even starting with 5-10 posts repurposed from podcast content
17. **SEO optimization** — Meta titles, descriptions, Open Graph tags for every page

### Major Rebuilds (High Effort, Transformative)

18. **Homepage redesign** — Current homepage undersells everything. Needs: hero with podcast stats, "as seen in" bar, featured episodes, testimonials, clear CTAs for coaching/community/podcast
19. **AI Consulting / Alset Technologies page** — Requires new content, potentially a sub-brand or integrated section
20. **Amakaya / Plant Medicine page** — Sensitive topic, requires careful messaging and positioning
21. **Full episode archive with transcripts** — 158 episodes with searchable transcripts would be an SEO goldmine
22. **Store / Products section** — Audiobook, coffee partnership, potential merchandise
23. **Newsletter archive** — Past issues available for browsing to demonstrate value before signup
24. **Community portal integration** — If The Stronghold has a platform, integrate signup/login from the website

---

## Part 6: Raw Research Notes

### All Sources Found

**Official Platforms:**
- Website: https://www.thestrategicveteran.com (Webflow-hosted, CDN: cdn.prod.website-files.com)
- Spotify: https://open.spotify.com/show/3U8FOupOu070V00PXR4DSd
- Apple Podcasts: https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran/id1743039436 (ID: 1743039436)
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@thestrategicveteran (Channel ID: UChSuB5bX_E-zXvi5yJ6R5vQ)
- Megaphone feed: https://feeds.megaphone.fm/the-strategic-veteran
- LinkedIn personal: https://www.linkedin.com/in/thestrategicveteran/
- LinkedIn company: https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-strategic-veteran/
- X/Twitter: https://x.com/thestrategicvet (@thestrategicvet)
- Instagram: @pete0585
- Facebook personal: https://www.facebook.com/pete.peters.39
- Facebook page: https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61563037727718
- TidyCal booking: https://tidycal.com/thestrategicveteran
- Email: support@thestrategicveteran.com
- Phone: 912-656-1917

**Affiliated Ventures:**
- Alset Technologies: https://alsettechnologies.com/ (Adam is CTO)
- Amakaya Retreat: https://www.amakaya.org/ (Adam is Partner)
- Heroes Media Group: https://www.heroesmediagroup.com/ (podcast production network)
- Ramshorn Coffee: https://ramshorncoffee.com/ (sponsor, code THESTRATEGICVET)

**Press/Features:**
- Bold Journey Magazine: https://boldjourney.com/meet-adam-peters/ (July 2024)
- CanvasRebel Magazine (1st): https://canvasrebel.com/meet-adam-peters/ (2024)
- CanvasRebel Magazine (2nd): https://canvasrebel.com/meet-adam-peters-2/ (Dec 2024)
- Umbrella Local Heroes: https://umbrellalocalheroes.com/interview-with-adam-peters-empowering-veterans-through-strategic-community-building/ (July 2024)
- Endure By Faith (partner listing): https://www.ebfllc.org/partners/podcasts

**Speaking Events:**
- VETSOCIAL25 LIVE: https://event.veteransocialsummit.com/speakers/adam-peters/
- 2025 Military Life Cycle and Transition Summit (University of Chicago / Boeing): https://veterans.uchicago.edu/news-and-events-1/events-2025-2026-ay/military-life-cycle-and-transition-summit-2025/
- The Speakers Collaborative (training program, mentioned in CanvasRebel)

**Podcast Guest Appearances (Adam as guest):**
- Thought Forge Network with Manuel Martinez (Sep 2, 2024) — 2hr 11min: https://creators.spotify.com/pod/profile/manuel-martinez505/episodes/Strategic-Veteran-Podcast-Adam-Peters-e2nti2v
- HR Payroll 2.0 with Pete & Julie (Sep 2025): https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-strategic-veteran-with-special-guest-adam-peters/id1643933833?i=1000728035154
- Civvies Podcast (Sep 11, 2025): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3AZixVQfpQ4
- VET S.O.S. Podcast (Nov 12, 2025): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bpIAMPBaoc0
- Unknown show — "Healing, Transition & Truth" (Aug 28, 2025): https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qh8CmQ1-ClE

**Podcast Analytics:**
- Rephonic profile: https://rephonic.com/podcasts/the-strategic-veteran
- 158 episodes as of audit date
- Launched ~2 years ago
- Recent guests include: David Puri, Benjamin Parmele, Georgia Lee Arts, Jason Anderson, Matt Payne, Buffy Payne, Chad Rarie, Richard Walsh, Jay Patterson, Joshua Palassis, Peter Pollock, Rand Timmerman, David Befort, Chad Cooper, and 140+ more

**Amazon/Book:**
- The Strategic Veteran audiobook: https://www.amazon.co.uk/The-Strategic-Veteran/dp/B0FLGKDF6R

**Podcast Directory Listings:**
- Parade Deck (Veteran Podcast Network): https://veteran-podcast-network.paradedeck.com/
- Rephonic: https://rephonic.com/podcasts/the-strategic-veteran

**Key Biographical Details Confirmed Across Multiple Sources:**
- Born and raised in southern Indiana
- Joined the Army after high school
- 13 years active duty service (2003-2015)
- Army infantry
- 4 combat deployments to Iraq and Afghanistan
- Medically retired
- Based in Willacoochee, GA (as of 2024)
- Founded TSV: November 2023
- Podcast launched: May 1, 2024
- Lost ~100 lbs starting July 7, 2023
- 9 surgeries
- Overcame alcohol dependency → food/sugar addiction → cannabis use → plant medicine transformation
- Ayahuasca ceremony was pivotal turning point
- Started with 93 LinkedIn followers
- Used Loom video DMs for authentic networking
- Participated in ACP (American Corporate Partners) mentorship
- Took and left a non-profit director role to refocus on TSV
- Pivoted The Stronghold from paid ($450 MRR) to free
- Launched AI automation service line
- Training in public speaking through The Speakers Collaborative

**Website Technical Details:**
- Platform: Webflow
- CDN: cdn.prod.website-files.com (Webflow CDN)
- Known pages: /, /about, /podcast, /podcast/[episode-slug], /newsletter, /contact
- No blog, no store, no services page, no speaking page, no community page

### Surprising Findings & Missed Opportunities

1. **158 episodes is a massive achievement** — Most podcasts don't survive past 7 episodes. TSV has published 158 long-form interviews. This is nowhere on the website.

2. **The University of Chicago/Boeing presentation is major credibility** — An academic institution + Fortune 50 company invited Adam to present. This should be front and center.

3. **The CAIO title is valuable and rare** — Chief AI Officer is a hot credential. Adam holds this title and it's not mentioned anywhere on TSV.

4. **The personal transformation story is the most compelling content** — Every single interview leads with Adam's transformation (100 lbs, ayahuasca, surgeries, addiction). The website barely hints at it.

5. **Heroes Media Group relationship** — Being part of HMG's network gives credibility. Not mentioned on site.

6. **Ramshorn Coffee partnership** — Active sponsor relationship with discount code. No presence on site.

7. **Adam has been featured in 4+ magazine-style publications** — Zero are referenced on the website.

8. **The "Unfuck Your Transition" positioning** — This may be used in LinkedIn/social media but is absent from the website. If this is the real brand voice, it should be reflected.

9. **Alset Technologies has its own website** — But no cross-linking or mention from TSV site.

10. **YouTube channel has a great description** — "Military Transition, Identity, and Purpose" — this could be the website's positioning statement.

11. **The newsletter claims readers from PGA of America, Diamond Sharp Capital, etc.** — These logos should appear elsewhere on the site, not buried on a signup page.

12. **No SEO play at all** — With 158 episodes of content, there's a massive opportunity for long-tail search traffic from episode transcripts, guest names, and topic keywords. Currently zero organic search presence beyond the 5 static pages.

---

## Appendix: Website Pages Inventoried

| URL | Status | Content Quality |
|---|---|---|
| thestrategicveteran.com/ | Live | Thin, outdated, undersells |
| thestrategicveteran.com/about | Live | Extremely thin (3 sentences) |
| thestrategicveteran.com/podcast | Live | Has testimonials, missing episode archive & stats |
| thestrategicveteran.com/podcast/[slug] | Live | Good format, no master directory |
| thestrategicveteran.com/newsletter | Live | Minimal, has logo social proof |
| thestrategicveteran.com/contact | Live | Bare form, no details |
| thestrategicveteran.com/speaking | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/coaching | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/ai-consulting | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/community | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/press | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/amakaya | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/blog | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/store | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/episodes | Missing | Needed |
| thestrategicveteran.com/about/adam | Missing | Needed |

---

*End of audit. This report captures the complete state of Adam Peters' online presence vs. his website as of March 4, 2026. Every finding is sourced from publicly available information.*

content.content: Launching skill: llm-secrets

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /job/.claude/skills/llm-secrets

# List Available Secrets

```bash
skills/llm-secrets/llm-secrets.js
```

Shows the names of available secret keys (not values). Output example:

```
Available secrets:
  - BROWSER_PASSWORD
  - SOME_API_KEY

To get a value: echo $KEY_NAME
```

## Get a Secret Value

```bash
echo $KEY_NAME
```

Replace `KEY_NAME` with one of the available secret names.

## When to Use

- When a skill or tool needs authentication credentials
- When logging into a website via browser tools
- When calling an external API that requires a key

  - WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN

To get a value: echo $KEY_NAME

  - WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN

To get a value: echo $KEY_NAME

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# List all pages on the Webflow site
set -euo pipefail

SITE_ID="66878d6a1082f3dca2c24ae6"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  "$API_BASE/sites/$SITE_ID/pages" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .

1→---
     2→name: browser-tools
     3→description: Interactive browser automation via Chrome DevTools Protocol. Use when you need to interact with web pages, test frontends, or when user interaction with a visible browser is required.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Browser Tools
     7→
     8→Chrome DevTools Protocol tools for agent-assisted web automation. These tools connect to Chrome running on `:9222` with remote debugging enabled.
     9→
    10→## Setup
    11→
    12→Run once before first use:
    13→
    14→```bash
    15→cd skills/browser-tools
    16→npm install
    17→```
    18→
    19→## Start Chrome
    20→
    21→```bash
    22→skills/browser-tools/browser-start.js              # Fresh profile
    23→skills/browser-tools/browser-start.js --profile    # Copy user's profile (cookies, logins)
    24→```
    25→
    26→Launch Chrome with remote debugging on `:9222`. Use `--profile` to preserve user's authentication state.
    27→
    28→## Navigate
    29→
    30→```bash
    31→skills/browser-tools/browser-nav.js https://example.com
    32→skills/browser-tools/browser-nav.js https://example.com --new
    33→```
    34→
    35→Navigate to URLs. Use `--new` flag to open in a new tab instead of reusing current tab.
    36→
    37→## Evaluate JavaScript
    38→
    39→```bash
    40→skills/browser-tools/browser-eval.js 'document.title'
    41→skills/browser-tools/browser-eval.js 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'
    42→```
    43→
    44→Execute JavaScript in the active tab. Code runs in async context. Use this to extract data, inspect page state, or perform DOM operations programmatically.
    45→
    46→## Screenshot
    47→
    48→```bash
    49→skills/browser-tools/browser-screenshot.js
    50→```
    51→
    52→Capture current viewport and return temporary file path. Use this to visually inspect page state or verify UI changes.
    53→
    54→## Pick Elements
    55→
    56→```bash
    57→skills/browser-tools/browser-pick.js "Click the submit button"
    58→```
    59→
    60→**IMPORTANT**: Use this tool when the user wants to select specific DOM elements on the page. This launches an interactive picker that lets the user click elements to select them. The user can select multiple elements (Cmd/Ctrl+Click) and press Enter when done. The tool returns CSS selectors for the selected elements.
    61→
    62→Common use cases:
    63→- User says "I want to click that button" → Use this tool to let them select it
    64→- User says "extract data from these items" → Use this tool to let them select the elements
    65→- When you need specific selectors but the page structure is complex or ambiguous
    66→
    67→## Cookies
    68→
    69→```bash
    70→skills/browser-tools/browser-cookies.js
    71→```
    72→
    73→Display all cookies for the current tab including domain, path, httpOnly, and secure flags. Use this to debug authentication issues or inspect session state.
    74→
    75→## Extract Page Content
    76→
    77→```bash
    78→skills/browser-tools/browser-content.js https://example.com
    79→```
    80→
    81→Navigate to a URL and extract readable content as markdown. Uses Mozilla Readability for article extraction and Turndown for HTML-to-markdown conversion. Works on pages with JavaScript content (waits for page to load).
    82→
    83→## When to Use
    84→
    85→- Testing frontend code in a real browser
    86→- Interacting with pages that require JavaScript
    87→- When user needs to visually see or interact with a page
    88→- Debugging authentication or session issues
    89→- Scraping dynamic content that requires JS execution
    90→
    91→---
    92→
    93→## Efficiency Guide
    94→
    95→### DOM Inspection Over Screenshots
    96→
    97→**Don't** take screenshots to see page state. **Do** parse the DOM directly:
    98→
    99→```javascript
   100→// Get page structure
   101→document.body.innerHTML.slice(0, 5000)
   102→
   103→// Find interactive elements
   104→Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button, input, [role="button"]')).map(e => ({
   105→  id: e.id,
   106→  text: e.textContent.trim(),
   107→  class: e.className
   108→}))
   109→```
   110→
   111→### Complex Scripts in Single Calls
   112→
   113→Wrap everything in an IIFE to run multi-statement code:
   114→
   115→```javascript
   116→(function() {
   117→  // Multiple operations
   118→  const data = document.querySelector('#target').textContent;
   119→  const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button');
   120→  
   121→  // Interactions
   122→  buttons[0].click();
   123→  
   124→  // Return results
   125→  return JSON.stringify({ data, buttonCount: buttons.length });
   126→})()
   127→```
   128→
   129→### Batch Interactions
   130→
   131→**Don't** make separate calls for each click. **Do** batch them:
   132→
   133→```javascript
   134→(function() {
   135→  const actions = ["btn1", "btn2", "btn3"];
   136→  actions.forEach(id => document.getElementById(id).click());
   137→  return "Done";
   138→})()
   139→```
   140→
   141→### Typing/Input Sequences
   142→
   143→```javascript
   144→(function() {
   145→  const text = "HELLO";
   146→  for (const char of text) {
   147→    document.getElementById("key-" + char).click();
   148→  }
   149→  document.getElementById("submit").click();
   150→  return "Submitted: " + text;
   151→})()

   153→

file.content:
---
name: browser-tools
description: Interactive browser automation via Chrome DevTools Protocol. Use when you need to interact with web pages, test frontends, or when user interaction with a visible browser is required.
---

# Browser Tools

Chrome DevTools Protocol tools for agent-assisted web automation. These tools connect to Chrome running on `:9222` with remote debugging enabled.

## Setup

Run once before first use:

```bash
cd skills/browser-tools
npm install
```

## Start Chrome

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-start.js              # Fresh profile
skills/browser-tools/browser-start.js --profile    # Copy user's profile (cookies, logins)
```

Launch Chrome with remote debugging on `:9222`. Use `--profile` to preserve user's authentication state.

## Navigate

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-nav.js https://example.com
skills/browser-tools/browser-nav.js https://example.com --new
```

Navigate to URLs. Use `--new` flag to open in a new tab instead of reusing current tab.

## Evaluate JavaScript

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-eval.js 'document.title'
skills/browser-tools/browser-eval.js 'document.querySelectorAll("a").length'
```

Execute JavaScript in the active tab. Code runs in async context. Use this to extract data, inspect page state, or perform DOM operations programmatically.

## Screenshot

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-screenshot.js
```

Capture current viewport and return temporary file path. Use this to visually inspect page state or verify UI changes.

## Pick Elements

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-pick.js "Click the submit button"
```

**IMPORTANT**: Use this tool when the user wants to select specific DOM elements on the page. This launches an interactive picker that lets the user click elements to select them. The user can select multiple elements (Cmd/Ctrl+Click) and press Enter when done. The tool returns CSS selectors for the selected elements.

Common use cases:
- User says "I want to click that button" → Use this tool to let them select it
- User says "extract data from these items" → Use this tool to let them select the elements
- When you need specific selectors but the page structure is complex or ambiguous

## Cookies

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-cookies.js
```

Display all cookies for the current tab including domain, path, httpOnly, and secure flags. Use this to debug authentication issues or inspect session state.

## Extract Page Content

```bash
skills/browser-tools/browser-content.js https://example.com
```

Navigate to a URL and extract readable content as markdown. Uses Mozilla Readability for article extraction and Turndown for HTML-to-markdown conversion. Works on pages with JavaScript content (waits for page to load).

## When to Use

- Testing frontend code in a real browser
- Interacting with pages that require JavaScript
- When user needs to visually see or interact with a page
- Debugging authentication or session issues
- Scraping dynamic content that requires JS execution

---

## Efficiency Guide

### DOM Inspection Over Screenshots

**Don't** take screenshots to see page state. **Do** parse the DOM directly:

```javascript
// Get page structure
document.body.innerHTML.slice(0, 5000)

// Find interactive elements
Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('button, input, [role="button"]')).map(e => ({
  id: e.id,
  text: e.textContent.trim(),
  class: e.className
}))
```

### Complex Scripts in Single Calls

Wrap everything in an IIFE to run multi-statement code:

```javascript
(function() {
  // Multiple operations
  const data = document.querySelector('#target').textContent;
  const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button');
  
  // Interactions
  buttons[0].click();
  
  // Return results
  return JSON.stringify({ data, buttonCount: buttons.length });
})()
```

### Batch Interactions

**Don't** make separate calls for each click. **Do** batch them:

```javascript
(function() {
  const actions = ["btn1", "btn2", "btn3"];
  actions.forEach(id => document.getElementById(id).click());
  return "Done";
})()
```

### Typing/Input Sequences

```javascript
(function() {
  const text = "HELLO";
  for (const char of text) {
    document.getElementById("key-" + char).click();
  }
  document.getElementById("submit").click();
  return "Submitted: " + text;
})()
```

### Reading App/Game State

Extract structured state in one call:

```javascript
(function() {
  const state = {
    score: document.querySelector('.score')?.textContent,
    status: document.querySelector('.status')?.className,
    items: Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('.item')).map(el => ({
      text: el.textContent,
      active: el.classList.contains('active')
    }))
  };
  return JSON.stringify(state, null, 2);
})()
```

### Waiting for Updates

If DOM updates after actions, add a small delay with bash:

```bash
sleep 0.5 && skills/browser-tools/browser-eval.js '...'
```

### Investigate Before Interacting

Always start by understanding the page structure:

```javascript
(function() {
  return {
    title: document.title,
    forms: document.forms.length,
    buttons: document.querySelectorAll('button').length,
    inputs: document.querySelectorAll('input').length,
    mainContent: document.body.innerHTML.slice(0, 3000)
  };
})()
```

Then target specific elements based on what you find.

content.thinking:
Chrome is running in headless mode now. Let me try to navigate and interact with the site using the browser tools scripts which should now be able to connect.Now I have Chrome running. But the fundamental problem remains: the Webflow API token lacks the necessary scopes to read/write pages. And I can't log into Webflow Designer through the browser because I don't have login credentials.

Let me re-think the approach. Since I cannot:
1. Use the Webflow API (missing scopes)
2. Log into Webflow Designer (no credentials)I have a few remaining options:
1. Use the browser to scrape the current site content, then report what needs to change


2. Build the content as HTML/markdown files that can be applied when the token is updated
3. Try to find if there's a Webflow login mechanism I'm missing

Actually, let me explore a different angle. Webflow has an "Editor" mode accessible through the live site with an edit URL, but that still requires authentication. Let me also check if the Webflow API v2 has endpoints for updating page SEO metadata that might have different scope requirements than full DOM access.

The errors are clear though: pages:read is needed for GET operations and pages:write is needed for POST/PUT operations, and both are missing from my current token. I'm wondering if I can access the Webflow Designer through its internal API instead—the Designer uses a different API than the public Data API, so maybe the OAuth token would work there. Or what if I leverage the Webflow CMS to create pages as CMS collection items with HTML content fields? That would make them accessible at /{slug} paths, but I'd need cms:read and cms:write scopes, which are also missing. I think I've hit the limits of what I can do with the current token, so let me explore one more option with the REST API.

Maybe I can pass the token as an API key in a header instead of as a Bearer token, or since this token starts with "ws-" (indicating it's a workspace/site token), there might be different authentication handling in the v2 API. I already tried the v1 format without success though. At this point, my best bet is probably to accept the API limitations, thoroughly scrape the current site content through the browser, prepare all the new content as files, and then figure out what additional scopes or approach I need. Actually, I wonder if I can modify the Webflow skill itself to work around these constraints.

No, that's getting too complicated. Let me check the Webflow API v2 documentation one more time—specifically the Pages endpoint—to see if there's something I've missed before I resort to creating a comprehensive changelist document.

content.content: Launching skill: brave-search

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /job/.claude/skills/brave-search

# Brave Search

Web search and content extraction using the official Brave Search API. No browser required.

## Setup

Requires a Brave Search API account with a free subscription. A credit card is required to create the free subscription (you won't be charged).

1. Create an account at https://api-dashboard.search.brave.com/register
2. Create a "Free AI" subscription
3. Create an API key for the subscription
4. Add to your shell profile (`~/.profile` or `~/.zprofile` for zsh):
   ```bash
   export BRAVE_API_KEY="your-api-key-here"
   ```
5. Install dependencies (run once):
   ```bash
   cd skills/brave-search
   npm install
   ```

## Search

```bash
skills/brave-search/search.js "query"                         # Basic search (5 results)
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" -n 10                   # More results (max 20)
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --content               # Include page content as markdown
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --freshness pw          # Results from last week
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --freshness 2024-01-01to2024-06-30  # Date range
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" --country DE            # Results from Germany
skills/brave-search/search.js "query" -n 3 --content          # Combined options
```

### Options

- `-n <num>` - Number of results (default: 5, max: 20)
- `--content` - Fetch and include page content as markdown
- `--country <code>` - Two-letter country code (default: US)
- `--freshness <period>` - Filter by time:
  - `pd` - Past day (24 hours)
  - `pw` - Past week
  - `pm` - Past month
  - `py` - Past year
  - `YYYY-MM-DDtoYYYY-MM-DD` - Custom date range

## Extract Page Content

```bash
skills/brave-search/content.js https://example.com/article
```

Fetches a URL and extracts readable content as markdown.

## Output Format

```
--- Result 1 ---
Title: Page Title
Link: https://example.com/page
Age: 2 days ago
Snippet: Description from search results
Content: (if --content flag used)
  Markdown content extracted from the page...

--- Result 2 ---
...
```

## When to Use

- Searching for documentation or API references
- Looking up facts or current information
- Fetching content from specific URLs
- Any task requiring web search without interactive browsing

Could not parse CSS stylesheet Could not parse CSS stylesheet Could not parse CSS stylesheet --- Result 1 --- Title: Webflow Designer API: Create page Link: https://developers.webflow.com/designer/reference/create-page Snippet: Create a new page in the Designer. This method will return an error if the App tries to create more pages than a User's plan allows. Content: ## `webflow.createPage()` Create a new page in the Designer. This method will return an error if the App tries to create more pages than a User’s plan allows. ### Syntax
1webflow.createPage(): Promise<Page>
### Returns **Promise<_Page_\>** A Promise that resolves to the created page. ### Example
1// Create new page and set page name
2const newPage = await webflow.createPage() as Page
3await newPage.setName('My New Page')
4
5// Switch to new page
6await webflow.switchPage(newPage)
### Designer Ability | Designer Ability | Locale | Branch | Workflow | Sitemode | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | **canCreatePage** | Any | Any | Any | Design | --- Result 2 --- Title: Update Page Content | Webflow Developer Documentation Link: https://developers.webflow.com/data/reference/pages-and-components/pages/update-static-content Snippet: </Note> Required scope | `pages:write` ... Bearer authentication of the form Bearer <token>, where token is your auth token. ... The locale identifier. This endpoint expects an object. ... List of DOM Nodes with the new content that will be updated in each node. ... This endpoint updates content on a static page in secondary locales. Content: This endpoint updates content on a static page in \*\*secondary locales\*\*. It supports updating up to 1000 nodes in a single request. Before making updates: 1. Use the \[get page content\](/data/reference/pages-and-components/pages/get-content) endpoint to identify available content nodes and their types. 2. If the page has component instances, retrieve the component's properties that you'll override using the \[get component properties\](/data/reference/pages-and-components/components/get-properties) endpoint. 3. DOM elements may include a \`data-w-id\` attribute. This attribute is used by Webflow to maintain custom attributes and links across locales. input.prompt:
Do TWO things:

## 1. Create `/job/data/webflow-pages/contact.html`

Full HTML content for the updated Contact page of thestrategicveteran.com.

Current state: bare contact form with no details. Needs expansion.

Content:
- Hero: "Get In Touch"
- Contact Details:
  - Email: support@thestrategicveteran.com
  - Booking: "Schedule a call" with link to tidycal.com/thestrategicveteran
- Social Media Links:
  - LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/thestrategicveteran
  - YouTube: youtube.com/@thestrategicveteran
  - X/Twitter: x.com/thestrategicvet
  - Instagram: @pete0585
  - Facebook: facebook.com/pete.peters.39
- Quick Links:
  - "Want to be a podcast guest?" -- link to /guest
  - "Interested in speaking?" -- link to /speaking
  - "Media inquiries?" -- link to /press
  - "AI consulting?" -- link to /ai-consulting
- Contact form (existing): Name, Email Address, Message
- NO references to The Stronghold, Alset Technologies, or CAIO
- No em dashes (use -- instead)

## 2. Create THREE new Webflow API scripts

These scripts extend the existing Webflow skill at `/job/skills/webflow/`.

### Script A: `/job/skills/webflow/get-page-dom.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Get the DOM content of a static Webflow page
# Usage: get-page-dom.sh <page-id>
# Requires: pages:read scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: get-page-dom.sh <page-id>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID/dom" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

### Script B: `/job/skills/webflow/update-page-dom.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Update the DOM content of a static Webflow page
# Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>
# The JSON file should contain a "nodes" array with content updates
# Requires: pages:write scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
JSON_FILE="${2:?Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$JSON_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: JSON file not found: $JSON_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @"$JSON_FILE" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID/dom" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

### Script C: `/job/skills/webflow/update-page-settings.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Update page settings (title, description, SEO metadata)
# Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>
# JSON should contain fields like: title, description, seo (object with title, description)
# Requires: pages:write scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
JSON_FILE="${2:?Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$JSON_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: JSON file not found: $JSON_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @"$JSON_FILE" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

Make sure all three scripts are executable (chmod +x). Write them exactly as shown above.

- Contact form (existing): Name, Email Address, Message
- NO references to The Stronghold, Alset Technologies, or CAIO
- No em dashes (use -- instead)

## 2. Create THREE new Webflow API scripts

These scripts extend the existing Webflow skill at `/job/skills/webflow/`.

### Script A: `/job/skills/webflow/get-page-dom.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Get the DOM content of a static Webflow page
# Usage: get-page-dom.sh <page-id>
# Requires: pages:read scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: get-page-dom.sh <page-id>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID/dom" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

### Script B: `/job/skills/webflow/update-page-dom.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Update the DOM content of a static Webflow page
# Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>
# The JSON file should contain a "nodes" array with content updates
# Requires: pages:write scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
JSON_FILE="${2:?Usage: update-page-dom.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$JSON_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: JSON file not found: $JSON_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @"$JSON_FILE" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID/dom" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

### Script C: `/job/skills/webflow/update-page-settings.sh`

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Update page settings (title, description, SEO metadata)
# Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>
# JSON should contain fields like: title, description, seo (object with title, description)
# Requires: pages:write scope
set -euo pipefail

PAGE_ID="${1:?Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
JSON_FILE="${2:?Usage: update-page-settings.sh <page-id> <json-file>}"
API_BASE="https://api.webflow.com/v2"

if [ -z "${WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
  echo "Error: WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN is not set" >&2
  exit 1
fi

if [ ! -f "$JSON_FILE" ]; then
  echo "Error: JSON file not found: $JSON_FILE" >&2
  exit 1
fi

response=$(curl -s -w "\n%{http_code}" \
  -X PUT \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $WEBFLOW_API_TOKEN" \
  -H "Accept: application/json" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d @"$JSON_FILE" \
  "$API_BASE/pages/$PAGE_ID" 2>&1) || {
  echo "Error: API request failed" >&2
  echo "$response" >&2
  exit 1
}

http_code=$(echo "$response" | tail -1)
body=$(echo "$response" | sed '$d')

if [ "$http_code" -ge 400 ]; then
  echo "Error: HTTP $http_code" >&2
  echo "$body" | jq . 2>/dev/null || echo "$body" >&2
  exit 1
fi

echo "$body" | jq .
```

Make sure all three scripts are executable (chmod +x). Write them exactly as shown above.
